scope.question: Seed source-rights classifications for the D+Q bone RCT registry and PubMed sources before retained text artifact ingestion.

inputs.summary: User requested a legal-risk mitigation layer using attribution, disclaimers, and process controls.
ClinicalTrials.gov registry source for the D+Q skeletal health trial.
PubMed-indexed Nature Medicine article source for the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT.
ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 snapshot linked by the public_registry rights classification.
PubMed EFetch snapshot linked by the metadata_only rights classification.

outputs.summary: Created a submitted source-rights seed candidate with one public_registry classification for the ClinicalTrials.gov source and one metadata_only classification for the PubMed/publisher article source.

proposed_records.rationale: Tracks the rights-classification seed records and required source-fidelity review lane.
Classifies the ClinicalTrials.gov registry source as public_registry with retained registry payload, normalized artifact, structured extraction, and manifest export permissions.
Classifies the PubMed/publisher article source as metadata_only until a reusable full-text or repository source is separately classified.

quality_checks.summary: Source-rights records use the new source_rights schema fields for attribution, terms source, allowed artifact classes, public export policy, and remediation policy.
The candidate declares source_fidelity as a required review lane before any rights classification can be promoted.
npm run verify:knowledge-base passed after source-rights export refresh.

next_actions: Run source-fidelity supervisor review for the source-rights candidate before promotion.
Use the ClinicalTrials.gov public_registry rights record for the first retained registry text-snapshot ingestion pass.
